verse.name: V1
verse.lines: That God should love A sinner such as I, Should yearn to change My sorrow into bliss, Nor rest till He Had planned to bring me nigh, How wonderful is love like this!

verse.name: C2
verse.lines: Such love, such wondrous love, Such love, such wondrous love, That God should love A sinner such as I, How wonderful is love like this!

verse.name: V3
verse.lines: That Christ should join So freely in the scheme, Although it meant His death on Calvary, Did ever human tongue Find nobler theme Than love divine That ransomed me?

verse.name: V4
verse.lines: That for a wilful Outcast such as I, The Father planned, The Saviour bled and died; Redemption for A worthless slave to buy, Who long had law And grace defied.

verse.name: V5
verse.lines: And now He takes me To His heart, a son, He asks me not To fill a servant's place; The "far-off country" Wand'rings all are done, Wide open are His arms of grace.
